INFS 3822 Artificial Intelligence for Business Analytics

Week 1 Seminar

Introduction to Artificial Intelligence Fundamental Concepts and Applications

Dr Jason Xianghua Wu School of Information Systems & Technology Management UNSW Business School  Week 1 Seminar Outline Introduction to the course

Team Expectations Structure Schedule Resources Assignments

Welcome! A Brief History of Artificial Intelligence (30 min) Pre-Seminar Activities Introduction to Machine Learning (60 min) Theory & Practice Introduction to Hands-On Artificial Intelligence Supervised Unsupervised Semi-supervised Reinforcement Deep Dive Train and test (70 min) an AI model using Current Machine Learning Landscape & Business Applications Teachable Machine (no coding required) Regression Classification Ranking Clustering Anomaly Generative Recommendation (45 min) Case Study detection AI

Cashier-free grocery stores

(35 min)  Teaching team

The Best Way to Contact Us

• Questions for the lecture, tutorial, assignments: Moodle → Course Announcements and Forums → Learning forums → Course Q&A

• Questions about yourself: Equitable Learning Plan (ELP), and other personal questions. Email LIC (jason.wu5@unsw.edu.au)!

About this course • This course is designed to challenge you and motivate you to develop critical thinking through a problem-solving approach • Students learn AI and ML analytical tools better when they are applied to real life examples • Hands-on activities and case studies on how to apply ML algorithms to conduct business analytics • The emphasis will be on understanding the basics of each ML technique • How to put ML algorithms into practice to solve business problems • What the results mean and what actionable insights you could derive from your analysis

Course Learning Outcomes • Demonstrate understanding of fundamentals of AI and ML • Apply ML techniques to solve business problems • Develop ML models to make predictions • Assess the accuracy of the performance of the ML models • Derive actionable insights/recommendations from the key findings • Consider the ethical implications when implementing ML models to conduct business analytics • Develop teamwork collaboration and gain critical thinking skills

Programming is NOT the focus of this course!

What to expect from this course (Theoretical knowledge)

A deep understanding of: • Various types of Machine Learning Problems

Classification Regression Clustering Anomaly detection

Data generation Recommendation Ranking

• Applicability of AI to a variety of business analytics problems

• Role of AI in supporting decisions making in a variety of industries

• Ethical Implications of AI-driven Business Analytics

What to expect from this course (Practical knowledge) • Hands-on experience with industry-grade AI tools

• Hands-on experience with AI tools in Python/R

or

Weekly course activities • Preparation Activities (60 min) Reading, watching videos, complete case study activities, configuring software needed for the lecture activities.

• Seminar: 3 hrs Theory & Knowledge (60 mins) Hands-On Activity (20 mins) • Quiz (preparation and participation) + Break (20 mins) • Case Study (30 mins) Hands-On Deep Dive (50 mins)

• Consultations: 1 hr per week Tuesday 4-5pm; On Teams (or by appointment: jason.wu5@unsw.edu.au). • Or Email the tutor to do the consultation if the question is from the tutorial sessions.

Weekly course activities To fully benefit from this course, you are encouraged • Complete the preparation activities before your arrival to class for the Seminar • Engage during the lecture & tutorial, and ask questions • Practice extensively the analysis outside the formal seminars to solidify your learning

Hands-On Deep Dives will be used to reinforce materials covered in Lecture • Very practical and designed to develop your analytical skills using plenty of exercises • Each week you will be given hands-on activities on analytical tasks to complete before/during tutorial sessions.

Evaluations (All Assignments will be out in Week 2)

What is Artificial Intelligence? “Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to the capability of a machine to imitate intelligent human behavior. This involves the development and application of algorithms and computational models that enable machines to perform tasks that would typically require human intelligence”

Russell, S., & Norvig, P. (2021). Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach, 4th Edition.

Artificial intelligence Machine learning ﻿ Neural networks Deep learning ﻿  Why AI-driven Business Analytics?

What is AI-driven Business Analytics?

Why AI-driven Business Analytics Matter? Big data has created a pressing need for advanced tools and techniques to process, analyse the vast amounts of data generated every day This is where Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) come into play! • Data-driven AI-driven Business Analytics relies on analysing huge amounts of data. The more data available, the more effective they are at producing accurate results. • Handling Complexity and Volume • It requires computer processing power to manage large datasets • Insights AI and ML can be leveraged to automate data analysis, • Identify patterns- trends, derive valuable and actionable insights • Predictive models create forecasts and predictions to identify potential risks/opportunities • Empower organisations plan for the future- make informed and timely decisions

Let’s travel back in time to explore the early days of AI!

A Brief History of Artificial Intelligence

The early days of AI Good Old-Fashioned Artificial Intelligence (GOFAI) 1950s The Turing Test - Alan Turing wrote a paper suggesting how to test a “thinking” machine and Development of Good Old-Fashioned Artificial 1960s Intelligence (GOFAI) including: • Symbolic AI (logic programming, production rules, semantic nets) • Approaches inspired by human or animal cognition or behaviour (Hodgkin-Huxley model of the brain as neurons forming an electrical network, with individual neurons firing in all-or-nothing (on/off) pulses) • Heuristics models (applied to search algorithms)

1962 CBS broadcast interviews The early days of AI with the AI pioneers: Jerome Wiesner, Oliver Selfridge, and Good Old-Fashioned Artificial Intelligence Claude Shannon (GOFAI) 1950s & 1960s

Source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aygSMgK3BEM  The early days of AI Before Siri and Alexa, Good Old-Fashioned Artificial Intelligence there was ELIZA (GOFAI) 1950s & 1960s

Source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RMK9AphfLco  The second first fall and fall and second thirdwave waveofofAI AI

1970s The First AI Winter - due to limitations of computer memory and processing speeds

The Era of Expert Systems - using the 1980s knowledge of experts to create systems that mimic the behaviour of human experts

Source: https://hackernoon.com/ai-in-medicine-a-beginners-guide-a3b34b1dd5d7

The second fall and third wave of AI

Source: https://hackernoon.com/ai-in-medicine-a-beginners-guide-a3b34b1dd5d7

The second fall and third wave of AI The Second AI Winter - due to 1990s limitations of Expert System computers, considered slow and clumsy Expert Systems were difficult to update, and could not “learn”

2010s The Era of Machine Learning The new building block for AI Applied to Natural Language Processing, Facial Recognition, Speech Recognition, Virtual Assistants (e.g. Siri, Google Assistant) Neural Networks get traction in Machine Learning Deep learning, a subset of machine learning, attempt to simulate the behaviour of the human brain

Source: https://hackernoon.com/ai-in-medicine-a-beginners-guide-a3b34b1dd5d7 26  The second fall and third wave of AI The Second AI Winter - due to Artificial Intelligence 1990s limitations of Expert System computers, considered slow and clumsy Machine Learning Expert Systems were difficult to update, and could not “learn” Neural Networks

2010s The Era of Machine Learning The new building block for AI Applied to Natural Language Processing, Facial Recognition, Speech Recognition, Deep Learning Virtual Assistants (e.g. Siri, Google Assistant) Neural Networks get traction in Machine Learning Deep learning, a subset of machine learning, attempt to simulate the behaviour of the human brain

27 27   The focus of this course is on the third wave of AI, “Machine Learning”, applied to Business Analytics

Let’s deep dive into Machine Learning!

What is Machine Learning? Machine learning is a subfield of AI can be defined as developing algorithms/models to learn from data More Data

Data Machine Machine Actionable Insights, Learning Learning Model Predictions Algorithm

Types of Machine Learning

Supervised Unsupervised Semi-supervised Reinforcement Machine Learning Machine Learning Machine Learning Machine learning

Supervised Machine Learning • Trained on labelled data • Task-Driven (Predict an outcome variable)

Current Machine Learning Landscape

Unsupervised/Supervised Machine Learning Supervised Machine Learning Unsupervised Machine Learning

Regression & Classification Clustering Anomaly detection Time-series forecasting

Predict numerical values Classify data into categories Categorise data in similar Find the outliers groupings or clusters (unexpected events/items) Examples of binary in a dataset classification: Success/Failure

Ranking Generative AI Recommendation Recommend a choice Order the results of a request or a Produce various types of content including text, images, e.g. “next item” to buy, “next query based on some criteria videos, audio, and synthetic data video” to watch, “next song” to listen to  Regression

Machine Learning method Business applications Predict numerical values • Forecasting sales

e.g. prices, #likes, #shares, age, distance, etc. • Cash forecasting

• Stock predictions

• Predicting clickthrough rate

Regression Applied to Hospitality/Tourism

Help loyal customers get the best value from their reward points

Predict reward points needed to get free/discounted rooms

Regression Source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9ceEMey4GXE

Classification

Machine Learning method Business applications Classify data into categories (or classes) • Sentiment analysis (Positive/Neutral/Negative)

• 2 classes = Binary classification • Document classification

• > 2 classes = Multinomial classification • Spam filtering

• Image classification

• Customer purchase/churn prediction

• Credit card fraud detection

• Credit-worthiness assessment

Classification Applied to Food Retail Stores

Help reduce defective products from being shipped to customers

Classify images of produce as as good or bad

Binary classification applied in image processing

Source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PKRglPS6IUo

Ranking

Machine Learning method Business applications Order the results of a request or a query • Ranking search results based on specific criteria • Websites ranked based on user search, preferences

• Products ranked based on user past purchases, increasing purchase

• Ranking music tracks (e.g. Spotify) based on user past listening activities

Ranking Applied to Social Media

Deliver relevant content to users

Rank content based on users’ past engagement and preferences

Ranking applied in content delivery Source: https://www.facebook.com/Engineering/videos/2883326508655039/

Clustering

Machine Learning method Business applications Categorise data points in similar groupings • Customer Segmentation to group them into or clusters clusters based on purchase behaviour

• Movies recommendation to group users based on viewing habits/preferences

• Grouping product usage patterns

• Grouping similar products types into product categories

Clustering Applied to the Energy Sector

Help customers better understand their energy usage per appliance

Group similar energy usage patterns together and associate them with an appliance

Clustering applied in signal processing Source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=F9D__pRGM6A

Anomaly Detection

Machine Learning method Business applications Find the outliers (unexpected events/items) • Fraud detection in financial transactions in a dataset • Fault detection in manufacturing

• Intrusion detection in a computer network

• Spotting potential risks or medical problems in health data

Anomaly Detection Applied to Vehicle Inspection

Customers conduct automated vehicle inspections to identify anomalies and detect potential issues/faults

Detect anomalies in images of the vehicle (underbody, tires, etc.)

Anomaly detection applied in image processing Source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TizRyETGZbw

Generative AI

Machine Learning method Business applications Generate new content such as text, images, • Create marketing content using AI videos, articles, music, etc. • Generate short summaries of long documents

• Generate fake reviews to learn how to better detect them

• Generate medical reports, summarise patient data,

• Produce music, write song lyrics based on specific styles/themes, etc.

Generative AI applied to visual art

Capability Extend an image beyond its original borders “Creative AI”

Business applications Graphic designers design images and generate more content using AI

Free AI tools to create AI art

Business applications Movie producers generate videos from scripts using AI

Prompt: A stylish woman walks down a Tokyo street filled with warm glowing neon and animated city signage. She wears a black leather jacket, a long red dress, and black boots, and carries a black purse. She wears sunglasses and red lipstick. She walks confidently and casually. The street is damp and reflective, creating a mirror effect of the colorful lights. Many pedestrians walk about. Source: https://openai.com/index/sora/  Recommendation
